name: Apple Latkes
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons brandy, dark rum, or fruit liqueur
Vegetable oil for frying, preferably sunflower
recipeInstructions:
1. Core and peel the apples, then cut each into 4 thick slices.
2. In a shallow dish, mix the apple slices with sugar and brandy/rum/liqueur. Turn to coat well; let stand at least 1 hour, turning occasionally.
3. For the batter: Beat egg yolks with oil and salt, stir in flour, then gradually beat in water until smooth.
4. Allow batter to rest for an hour before adding stiffly beaten egg whites.
5. Heat at least 3/4 inch of vegetable oil in a large frying pan.
6. Dip apple slices into the batter (about 5 at a time), ensuring they are well covered.
7. Carefully lower each slice into the hot oil; avoid overheating, which can cause browning before the apples are tender.
8. Fry in batches, turning slices to brown on both sides.
9. Remove with a slotted spatula and drain on paper towels.
10. Sprinkle with superfine sugar before serving.
Yield: 4 servings
